questions.question: Explain the four signal lines used in SPI communication and their functions.
expectedPoints: MOSI: Master Out Slave In - data from master to slave
MISO: Master In Slave Out - data from slave to master
SCK: Serial Clock - synchronization signal generated by master
SS: Slave Select - active low signal to select specific slave

questions.question: Differentiate between SPI Mode 0 and Mode 3 in terms of clock polarity and phase.
expectedPoints: Mode 0: CPOL=0 (clock idle low), CPHA=0 (data sampled on rising edge)
Mode 3: CPOL=1 (clock idle high), CPHA=1 (data sampled on rising edge)
Both modes sample data on rising edge but have different clock idle states

questions.question: Compare SPI and I2C protocols in terms of speed, pin count, addressing, and multi-master support.
expectedPoints: Speed: SPI faster than I2C
Pin count: SPI requires more pins (3+n vs 2)
Addressing: SPI uses hardware addressing (SS lines), I2C uses software addressing
Multi-master: SPI doesn't support, I2C does support multi-master

questions.question: Explain the two main methods for connecting multiple slaves in an SPI system with diagrams.
expectedPoints: Independent Slave Select: Each slave has dedicated SS line from master, shared MOSI/MISO/SCK
Daisy-chain: Slaves connected in series, output of one feeds input of next
Advantages: Independent - simpler control, Daisy-chain - fewer pins
Disadvantages: Independent - many pins with many slaves, Daisy-chain - more complex data handling
Typical applications for each configuration

questions.question: Describe the step-by-step process of SPI data transmission with timing diagram.
expectedPoints: Master configures clock frequency and mode
Master pulls SS line low for target slave
Master generates clock pulses on SCK line
Data shifted out on MOSI and MISO simultaneously on each clock edge
After 8/16 clock pulses, complete byte/word transferred
Master raises SS line to end communication
Explanation of data sampling relative to clock edges based on CPHA
Diagram showing relationship between SCK, MOSI, MISO signals
